name: prisma-v7
description: Expert guidance for Prisma ORM v7 (7.0+). Use when working with Prisma schema files, migrations, Prisma Client queries, database setup, or when the user mentions Prisma, schema.prisma, @prisma/client, database models, or ORM. Covers ESM modules, driver adapters, prisma.config.ts, Rust-free client, and migration from v6.
capabilities: Generate and configure Prisma v7 client with new prisma-client provider
Set up driver adapters for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and other databases
Create and manage database migrations with Prisma v7 workflow
Write type-safe CRUD queries with Prisma Client including relations
Implement Prisma Accelerate for caching and connection pooling
Migrate existing Prisma v6 projects to v7 architecture
author: DjordjeVuckovic
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown content with Prisma ORM v7 guidance. No code execution, file access, network calls, or external commands. The static scanner misinterpreted markdown code blocks (backticks) as Ruby/shell execution, example process.env references as environment variable access, and example DATABASE_URL strings as credential access. All are documentation examples only, not actual executable code.
valueStatement: Building applications with Prisma ORM v7 requires understanding new ESM requirements, driver adapters, and configuration changes. This skill provides expert guidance on schema design, migrations, client queries, and migrating from v6 to v7.
userTitle: Master Prisma ORM v7 database development
limitations: Does not execute database queries or migrations directly
Does not provide live debugging of Prisma issues
Does not generate complete project scaffolds
Requires user to run Prisma CLI commands manually

useCases.title: Prisma v7 Setup
useCases.description: Configure new Prisma v7 projects with ESM, driver adapters, and proper client generation structure

useCases.title: Migration Planning
useCases.description: Plan and execute migrations from Prisma v6 to v7 including adapter migration and config changes

useCases.title: Query Optimization
useCases.description: Write efficient Prisma queries with relations, aggregations, transactions, and performance patterns

promptTemplates.title: Basic Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up a new Prisma v7 project with TypeScript and PostgreSQL including ESM configuration?

promptTemplates.title: Schema Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Prisma schema with User and Post models that have one-to-many relations and proper indexes

promptTemplates.title: Client Queries
promptTemplates.prompt: Write TypeScript code to query a user with all their posts using Prisma Client v7 with the include pattern

promptTemplates.title: Migration Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the exact steps to migrate from Prisma v6 to v7 including removing Rust dependencies and adding driver adapters?

bestPractices: Always run npx prisma generate after schema changes to update client types
Use select instead of include when you only need specific fields to improve performance
Create a singleton PrismaClient instance to avoid connection exhaustion in development
antiPatterns: Do not use url field in datasource block in v7 - move it to prisma.config.ts
Do not pass prisma:// URLs to driver adapters - use them only with Prisma Accelerate
Do not forget to add "type": "module" in package.json for ESM support

faq.question: Is Prisma v7 backward compatible with v6?
faq.answer: No, v7 requires ESM, driver adapters, and prisma.config.ts. Migration from v6 requires code changes.

faq.question: What databases does Prisma v7 support?
faq.answer: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, CockroachDB, PlanetScale, Neon, Cloudflare D1, and MSSQL.

faq.question: How do I configure the Prisma client in v7?
faq.answer: Use prisma.config.ts for database URL. Set generator provider to prisma-client with custom output path.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is read-only documentation. It provides guidance without accessing any databases or executing code.

faq.question: Why am I getting module resolution errors?
faq.answer: Ensure type module in package.json, run npx prisma generate, and verify output path matches your import.

faq.question: How does v7 differ from v6?
faq.answer: v7 requires ESM, removes Rust dependencies, uses driver adapters, and moves config to prisma.config.ts.
